LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS FILED IN SUPPORT OF CCSF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO SEAL THE DECLARATION OF SANDRA TONG IS GRANTED. THERE IS GOOD CAUSE TO SEAL THE CONFIDENTIAL PERSONNEL INFORMATION OF THE NON-PARTY. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AN ORDER THAT SETS FORTH THE FINDINGS OF CRC 2.550(D) AND ORDERS THE SANDRA TONG DECLARATION SEALED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Steven L. Robinson, Esq. (415.434.8700; srobinson@aliotolawoffice.com) of Law Offices of Joseph L. Alioto and Angela Alioto for Plaintiff.
Robert Rogoyski, Esq. (415.554.4284; robert.rogoyski@sfcityatty.org) of Deputy City Attorney for Defendant.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety.

Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production on the harassment claim. (See CCP 437c(p)(2).) Defendant's own motion demonstrates numerous incidents of misgendering, notice, a co-worker allegedly assaulting plaintiff, and a supervisor stating that he would not use preferred pronouns because it is "bad grammar." (See also Jones Decl., pars. 16-17.) There is a triable issue of material fact whether this conduct was sufficiently severe or pervasive to constitute harassment.

Moreover, the motion is denied because defendant's separate statement of undisputed facts is defective. First, defendant did not comply with CRC 3.1350(b) since the issues identified in the notice of motion are not repeated verbatim in the separate statement of undisputed facts. Second, the separate statement violates CRC 3.1350(d) ["(1) The Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in support of a motion must separately identify: (A) Each cause of action, claim for damages, issue of duty, or affirmative defense that is the subject of the motion; and (B) Each supporting material fact claimed to be without dispute with respect to the cause of action, claim for damages, issue of duty, or affirmative defense that is the subject of the motion."] In this case, defendant mixes up facts related solely to the discrimination or retaliation claims (e.g. facts regarding the failure to promote) with facts that relate solely to the harassment claims. Based on defendant's mash-up of facts, it is difficult to determine precisely which claims defendant seeks to summarily adjudicate. The court exercises its discretion based on defendant's failure to segregate the independent issues to deny the motion. (See Truong v. Glasser (2009) 181 Cal.App.4th 102, 118.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Brianna Rudd, CSR # 13668 (415.981.3498; briannarudd@gmail.com). =(302/RBU)
